tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated serum creatinine), hyperchloremia, and significant proteinuria with ketonuria and glucosuria, suggesting possible renal tubular dysfunction or early diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is low. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, raising suspicion for urinary tract pathology despite negative nitrites/leukocytes. Recommend further renal and urinary tract evaluation and clinical correlation for possible infection or metabolic derangement.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
